diagrams.title: Cache Memory Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between CPU, cache levels (L1, L2, L3), and main memory with speed/size indicators
diagrams.purpose: Understanding memory hierarchy and data flow

diagrams.title: Cache Address Decomposition
diagrams.description: 32-bit address divided into tag, index, and offset fields for cache access
elements: Cache line structure showing tag, valid bit, dirty bit, data
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how CPU addresses map to cache locations

diagrams.title: Direct Mapping Layout
diagrams.description: Each memory block maps to exactly one cache line based on index
elements: Mapping function: line = address mod number of lines
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing direct mapping and conflict issues

diagrams.title: Set-Associative Cache
diagrams.description: Cache organized into sets, each containing multiple ways/lines
elements: Within each set, any block can go in any way
Tag comparison done in parallel for all ways

diagrams.title: Cache Write Operations
diagrams.description: Comparison of write-through and write-back policies
diagrams.purpose: Understanding cache write behavior and data consistency
